your_book:
Friday was a split session for Jaleel's book, with the overall portfolio barely moving — unrealized gain settled at just $1.72 (+0.01%) on a $21,614 account. The standout mover on the upside was NVIDIA, which surged +4.03% to $1,376.66, extending its open gain to +15.0% and pulling the AI-chip thesis firmly back into the win column. Danaher added +1.57% and Lululemon bounced +2.36%, though LULU remains -6.7% in the hole with an active guidance-cut flag hanging over it — a bounce on an elevated-risk name deserves skepticism, not celebration. KO ticked up +1.04%, quiet and steady as expected.

The drags were meaningful. GILD fell -3.72% to $412.86, now -2.5% overall, and sits rated HOLD-QUAL with the MFN drug-pricing overhang unresolved — a small position but the day's worst performer by a wide margin. RKLB dropped another -1.83%, deepening its loss to -17.8%; it carries an AVOID verdict and the lowest QARP score in the book at 39.6, yet it continues to occupy 7.1% of capital. VRTX slipped -2.24% and Neurocrine -1.91%, both pulling back despite solid fundamentals and open gains.

The drift that warrants the most scrutiny remains ADBE — the largest single position at 16.2% ($3,503) and -8.8% in the red, yet still rated STRONG BUY with a QARP of 73.0. The narrative is clearly fighting the numbers there. BSX also drifts: STRONG BUY rated, 82.2 QARP, but guidance was slashed and the next earnings checkpoint is July 29. Watch whether the fundamental case gets confirmed or further eroded when that print lands.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated at 7.1% weight, down -17.8% and sinking
attention.note: RKLB holds the book's lowest QARP (39.6), carries an AVOID verdict, and dropped another -1.83% Friday — the position size is inconsistent with a capital-preservation mandate and there is no stated thesis offsetting the model's signal.

attention.headline: Largest position, -8.8% underwater, narrative overriding the numbers
attention.note: At 16.2% of the book ADBE is the single biggest risk concentration; Friday's +0.44% is noise against an -8.8% open loss, and the AI-disruption fear driving the selloff isn't resolving before the September 10 earnings date.

attention.headline: Worst day in the book (-3.72%) with drug-pricing policy risk live
attention.note: GILD dropped -3.72% Friday and sits HOLD-QUAL with MFN pricing agreements already signed — the July 30 policy checkpoint could add further pressure on a name the model doesn't rate highly.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, earnings July 29 — thesis needs confirmation
attention.note: BSX carries an elevated risk flag (FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5%, flat guided through Q3) and slipped -0.49% Friday; the July 29 print is the next hard test of whether the STRONG BUY case still holds.
